steps.title: Find the vertex x-coordinate from the roots
steps.content: Since the quadratic has roots at $x = -20$ and $x = -4$, the x-coordinate of the vertex lies on the axis of symmetry—which is always the midpoint between the two roots. Calculate directly: $$r = \frac{-20 + (-4)}{2} = \frac{-24}{2} = -12$$
